Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-22 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker-long Today's data reveals two exceptionally strong long-term performers at the top of the ranking. CVE (rank 1, score 92.2) shows explosive recent momentum with 126.62% one-year returns and 262.48% five-year returns, though notably it has dramatically underperformed over ten years (-160.15% vs SPY), suggesting a recent reversal in a historically challenged asset. EACO (rank 2) displays even more extreme long-term performance with a stunning 1758.23% ten-year return, crushing SPY by 1494% over that period, though its near-term momentum ratios are weaker than CVE's (0.85 three-month-to-one-month ratio vs CVE's 2.44). Both stocks pass all four moving average filters and show strong "clean" momentum signals, indicating they're trading above their technical support levels. The correlation data shows CVE is highly tied to energy sector movements (XLE correlation of 0.738), while EACO appears to be a specialized asset with strong absolute returns rather than sector-driven momentum. These rankings suggest the long-term scorer is identifying secular outperformers, though the extreme valuations in EACO's ten-year history warrant caution about mean reversion risk.
